author: Kahan Chand, Babu.
title: Bahadur Ki Talwar. The sword of a brave man.
gloss: A description of a fight between the Rajput army of the Raja of Gauri Garh and an independent Muhammadan Governor of Gujrat. Autar Singh, the Rajput hero, won the day and killed the Tartar Commander of the Muslim forces.

author: Kesho Ram, Lala.
title: Char Doston ki Dil Lagi.
gloss: The story of a resourceful woman who managed to preserve her chastity in adverse circumstances.

author: Muhammad Yusuf, Sheikh.
title: Sau Mangla, A pamphlet
gloss: in which the writer tries to refute the doctrine of transmigration of souls and describes its evil effects in the form of a novel. Sau Mangla is depicted as an innocent school girl who falls a victim to the wiles of a Pandit, a teacher, who under the cover of the doctrine of the transmigration of souls seduces her.

author: Sayad Ahmad, Munshi.
title: Qissa-i-Mihr Afroz.
gloss: Story of Mihr Afroz who is represented as a lady of noble family, generous and kind-hearted but illiterate and a spendthrift. Her younger sister Khirad Afroz was on the other hand an educated lady. Each sister had a son and a daughter, who married eachother respectively, and the spoilt children of the elder sister were reformed through the influence of the younger.

author: Shamsher Singh, Bhai, Khanjar.
title: Shahadat-i-Azim
gloss: or a description of the murder of two sons of Guru Gobind Singh, the 10th Sikh Guru, by the orders of the Subedar (ruler) of Sirhind.
publisher: B. Chatar Singh, Jiwan Singh, Booksellers

author: Tara Chand, Lala.
title: Muraqqa-i- Punjab, or a description
gloss: of the downfall of the Sikh rule in the Punjab, Part I.
copyright: Lala Tara Chand, Vakil Bikanir, Bahawalpur, No. 54, dated the 23rd May, 1911.

author: Lakhmi Das, Lala.
title: Asli Tawarikh, Arorbans.
gloss: A pamphlet tracing the history of Arorbans from 1412 A. D.
